name: Heart of Palm Pickles
recipeInstructions:
1. Ensure all tough fiber is trimmed from the heart of palm.
2. Cut the tender white portion into small strips.
3. Place these strips in a bowl of salted water (mix 2 tablespoons of salt with 1 quart of cold water) and let sit at cool room temperature for two days.
4. On the morning of the third day, discard the saltwater and wash the palm pieces well in cold water, then drain thoroughly.
5. In a large pot, mix together mustard seed, dry mustard, granulated sugar, turmeric, flour, red pepper, and cider vinegar until smooth.
6. Slowly bring to a boil while stirring with a wooden spoon. Stir continuously for about 10 minutes.
7. Add the lemon juice and rind (sliced into tiny slivers) and continue boiling for an additional 2 minutes.
8. Remove from heat and add the palm pieces, returning them to a boil briefly.
9. Ladle hot pickles into sterilized pint jars, leaving some headspace, then seal while still hot.
From Our Best Recipes by Lena E. Sturges, Food Editor. Birmingham, AL: Oxmoor House, Inc., 1970. Pg. 184. Library of Congress Catalog Number 70-140493.
